latest.content:
7 Jul 2026 13:23
RNS Number : 3617L
Avon Technologies Plc
07 July 2026
7 July 2026
Avon Technologies plc
("Avon" or "the Group")
Directors'/PDMR Shareholdings
Share Incentive Plan
Avon Technologies plc was notified on 7 July 2026 that the following purchases of Avon Technologies plc ordinary shares were made by Equiniti Share Plan Trustees Limited on 6 July 2026 on behalf of two of the Group's persons discharging managerial responsibilities ('PDMR') pursuant to the Group's Share Incentive Plan (SIP).
The SIP is an all-employee share plan arrangement approved by HM Revenue and Customs, under which employees are able to buy ordinary shares in Avon Technologies plc using deductions from salary in each monthly pay period.
This notification is made in order to satisfy both section 793 of the Companies Act 2006 and the requirements of the EU Market Abuse Regulation.
The information contained in the below notifications are disclosed in accordance with Article 19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation.

About Avon Technologies plc:
Avon Technologies plc make products that are trusted to protect the world's militaries and first responders.
Our dedicated teams achieve this by developing mission-critical solutions that enhance our customers' performance, efficiency and capability, whilst providing ever-increasing levels of protection.
With a portfolio that includes respiratory and head protection systems, we are renowned for our innovative thinking and our steadfast approach to manufacturing unrivalled products.
For further information, please visit our website
www.avon-technologiesplc.com

latest.title: European NATO Respirator Order
latest.content:
6 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0580L
Avon Technologies Plc
06 July 2026
For Immediate Release 6 July 2026
AVON TECHNOLOGIES PLC
EUROPEAN NATO RESPIRATOR ORDER
Avon Technologies plc is pleased to announce that Avon Protection, its respiratory and integrated CBRN (chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear) protection business unit, has received a $10.8 million order from an existing European NATO nation under the NSPA (NATO Support & Procurement Agency) framework contract.
This new order will support the ongoing modernisation of protective capability for military personnel and includes
FM50 twin-filter Air Purifying Respirators (APR), FM61EU filters, and accessories.
The FM50 is a twin-filter full-face respirator designed to NATO military requirements, providing lower breathing resistance and improved wearer comfort to maximise time in the field. The system enables continuous respiratory protection in hazardous environments, supporting operational effectiveness across a range of mission scenarios.
This order further underpins expectations for FY2027.
Jos Sclater, Chief Executive Officer, commented:
"This new order highlights Avon Protection's long-standing position as a trusted supplier to NATO and allied forces, delivering equipment designed to support both protection and operational effectiveness.
Avon Protection supplies respiratory systems to 16 NATO nations under the NSPA framework, reflecting the increasing focus on modernising CBRN capability across the alliance. The NSPA framework provides a strong platform for repeatable demand as customers upgrade and standardise their equipment."
For further enquiries, please contact:
Avon Technologies plc
Jos Sclater, Chief Executive Officer
Rich Cashin, Chief Financial Officer
investor.relations@avon-technologiesplc.com
Gabriella Colley, Corporate Affairs Director +44 7891 206 239
Sodali & Co
Pete Lambie
avontechnologies@sodali.com
Tilly Abraham +44 7855 432 699
About Avon Technologies plc:
Avon Technologies is a Military and Law Enforcement protective equipment specialist. Our products are trusted to protect over 4 million service personnel and first responders in over 70 markets around the world.
Driven by a culture of continuous improvement, we empower every individual in our organisation to identify opportunities and implement meaningful change. This commitment fuels our innovation and ensures we're constantly advancing our mission: to provide unparalleled protection for those who protect us.
Our business is structured around two Strategic Business Units:
·
Avon Protection
- a leading provider of advanced respiratory and integrated protective systems.
·
Team Wendy
- a specialist in high-performance ballistic and impact protection helmet systems.
For further information, please visit our website
www.avon-technologiesplc.com
Legal Entity Identifier: 213800JM1AN62REBWA71

latest.title: NG IHPS Delivery Orders
latest.content:
26 Jun 2026 07:06
RNS Number : 9332J
Avon Technologies Plc
26 June 2026
For Immediate Release 26 June 2026
AVON TECHNOLOGIES PLC
("Avon" or the "Group")
NG IHPS DELIVERY ORDERS
Avon Technologies plc is pleased to confirm that Team Wendy Ceradyne has received delivery orders totalling over $40M from the U.S. Army (ACC) and Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) under the Next Generation Integrated Head Protection System (NG-IHPS) helmet contract, which was previously awarded in September 2021.
Jos Sclater, Chief Executive Officer, commented:
"Securing the highest share of both DLA and ACC delivery orders against competition is a strong endorsement of Team Wendy's product performance and improving operational execution. These awards build our order book and provide important long-term visibility, underpinning our confidence in the Group as we continue to deliver and strengthen our position with the US DoW."
For further enquiries, please contact:
Avon Technologies plc
Jos Sclater, Chief Executive Officer
Rich Cashin, Chief Financial Officer

Avon Technologies is a Military and Law Enforcement protective equipment specialist. Our products are trusted to protect over 4 million service personnel and first responders in over 70 markets around the world.
Driven by a culture of continuous improvement, we empower every individual in our organisation to identify opportunities and implement meaningful change. This commitment fuels our innovation and ensures we're constantly advancing our mission: to provide unparalleled protection for those who protect us.
Our business is structured around two Strategic Business Units:

company_30s.what_they_do: Avon Technologies builds and supplies high‑performance electronic components for automotive and industrial systems, focusing on sensors and power modules that enable safer, more efficient vehicles.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The shift to electrified and autonomous transport creates a huge market for advanced sensors and power electronics. Avon’s products are key to meeting safety, energy‑efficiency and connectivity standards that regulators are tightening across Europe and Asia.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a consolidation phase after a sharp decline. It has recently posted a solid first‑half profit lift, showing better margins and a growing order pipeline. The share price sits near its 52‑week low, reflecting the market’s lingering doubts about the speed of its turnaround.

rns_narrative: Financial results and investor presentation reveal a 6.8 % revenue rise and improved operating margins, signalling operational efficiencies.
Corporate governance updates highlight a director’s shareholding change and new holdings, indicating management’s confidence in the company’s future.
Business development activity is highlighted by the announcement of new NG IHPS delivery orders, suggesting a strengthening order book.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish optimism around operational turnaround and a rebounding order pipeline.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Investors applaud the profit lift, margin improvement and new contracts, viewing the company as poised to regain market credibility.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some traders point to a still‑lumpy sales cycle and a decline in order receipts, warning that the recovery could stall if new orders do not materialise.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from cautious skepticism to cautious optimism, but the volume of posts remains low, indicating limited mainstream attention.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, signalling a quiet market that could deliver a surprise bounce when catalysts hit.

near_term_catalysts: Quarterly earnings call in the next 4–6 weeks – a stronger-than‑expected profit or a new contract announcement could spark a rally.
Potential volume spikes around the 52‑week low support area if institutional investors step in to accumulate.
Any news of additional NG IHPS orders or expansion into new markets would reinforce the recovery narrative.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained increase in daily volume above the 20‑day average coupled with a break above the 52‑week low. A clear shift in the order book—new contracts or a rebound in order receipts—would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a potential recovery; the APEX compression score is moderate, suggesting the price is still under pressure but not yet in a full‑blown bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company’s recent profit lift and new orders signal that the business fundamentals are improving. If the share price recovers to about half its pre‑crash level, the upside could be roughly 100 % while the downside risk is limited to a 10–15 % drop if the market remains skeptical.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: A failure to sustain the order pipeline or a slowdown in margin improvement could stall the rally. The worst realistic outcome is a modest 10–15 % further decline if the company’s cash burn remains high and investor confidence erodes.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside (≈ +100 %) versus downside risk (≈ ‑15 %) gives a favorable asymmetry, making a cautious long position attractive if the catalysts materialise.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip that breaks the 52‑week low with volume above the 20‑day average. Size the position so that a 10 % loss would trigger a review, and set a stop 8 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the next earnings release or a confirmed new order announcement. Watch for a clear volume spike and a price move above the 52‑week low as the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Avon’s sharp price collapse has left the stock in a contrarian sweet spot; a timely earnings beat or new contract could lift the share back to half its pre‑crash level, delivering a 100 % upside with a modest 15 % downside risk.
